name: Housecall Pro API Terms of Service

snapshot.summary: By accessing or using Housecall Pro APIs, you agree to the following TERMS OF SERVICE
snapshot.data:
--- stoplight-id: k5n8spwqgr3g5 --- # Housecall Pro API Terms of Service By accessing or using Housecall Pro APIs, you agree to the following TERMS OF SERVICE These Terms of Service (“Agreement”) constitute a legal agreement between you ("you" or “API Developer”) and Codefied Inc. d/b/a Housecall Pro and its affiliates, parents, and subsidiaries (collectively, "HCP" or "us"). This Agreement governs your use of our APIs, other developer services, and associated software (collectively, the “API Program” or “APIs”) which has associated documentation located at https://docs.housecallpro.com (the “API Docs”) and Housecall Pro may modify this Agreement and the associated documentation from time to time. By accessing or using our APIs, you are agreeing to the terms herein, and any amendments to this Agreement by your continued participation in the API Program. ## Definitions “Pros”: Customers of Housecall Pro “Integration”: The connection between Housecall Pro and any third party application or software via API that allows them to exchange data. ## Terms 1\) By accessing, using or registering with the API Program, you hereby expressly acknowledge and agree to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Agreement, and any future amendments and additions to this Agreement as we may publish from time to time. Please read this Agreement carefully. If you do not agree to accept and be bound by this Agreement, you must immediately stop using the Housecall Pro API Program. 2\) By using the API Program, you represent and warrant that: (i) you are at least 18 years old, are at least of the legally required age in the jurisdiction in which you reside, and are otherwise capable of entering into binding contracts; (ii) you have the right, authority and capacity to enter into this Agreement and to abide by the terms and conditions of this Agreement, and that you will so abide, (iii) you are authorized, and have all necessary rights and consents to utilize the API Program, (iv) you have been invited and approved by HCP for use of the API Program and (v) your use of the API Program is solely for the purpose of building Integrations for yourself or your customers, and not for the benefit of any other entities, nor shall you provide or authorize access to or use of the API Program by any third parties. When you enter into this Agreement on behalf of a company or other organization, you represent and warrant that you have authority to act on behalf of that entity and to bind that entity to this Agreement. 3\) Using Our APIs
    a\) Your End Users. You will be responsible for your end users compliance with applicable laws, regulations, and this Agreement.
    b\) Compliance with Law, Third Party Rights, and Other Housecall Pro Terms of Service. You will comply with all applicable law, regulation, and third party rights (including without limitation laws regarding the import or export of data or software, privacy, and local laws). You will not use the APIs to encourage or promote illegal activity or violation of third party rights. You will not violate any other terms of service with Housecall Pro (or its affiliates).
    c\) Permitted Access. You will only access (or attempt to access) an API by the means described in the documentation of that API. If Housecall Pro assigns you developer credentials (e.g. client IDs), you must use them with the applicable APIs. You will not misrepresent or mask either your identity or the end user’s identity when using the APIs or developer accounts.
    d\) API Limitations. Housecall Pro may set and enforce limits on your use of the APIs (e.g. limiting the number of API requests that you may make or the number of users you may serve), in our sole discretion. You agree to, and will not attempt to circumvent, such limitations documented with each API. If you would like to use any API beyond these limits, you must obtain Housecall Pro’s express consent (and we may decline such request or condition acceptance on your agreement to additional terms and/or charges for that use). To seek such approval, contact apideveloper@housecallpro.com
    e\) Feedback. If you provide feedback or suggestions about our APIs, then we (and those we allow) may use such information without obligation to you.
    f\) Non-Exclusivity. This Agreement is non-exclusive. You acknowledge that Housecall Pro may develop products or services that may compete with you or your Integration or any other products or services. 4\) Your API Integration
    a\) Monitoring. YOU AGREE THAT HCP MAY MONITOR USE OF THE APIs TO ENSURE QUALITY, IMPROVE HCP PRODUCTS AND SERVICES, AND VERIFY YOUR COMPLIANCE WITH THIS AGREEMENT. This monitoring may include HCP accessing and using your Integration, for example to identify security issues that could affect HCP or its users. You will not interfere with this monitoring. HCP may use any technical means to overcome such interference. HCP may suspend access to the APIs by you or your Integration without notice if we reasonably believe that you are in violation of this Agreement.
    b\) Security. You will use commercially reasonable efforts to protect user information collected by your Integration, including personal data, from unauthorized access or use and will promptly report to your users any unauthorized access or use of such information to the extent required by applicable law.
    c\)Ownership. HCP does not acquire ownership in your Integration, and by using our APIs, you do not acquire ownership of any rights in our APIs or the content that is accessed through our APIs.
    d\)User Privacy and Integration. You will comply with (1) all applicable privacy laws and regulations including those applying to personal data and You will provide and adhere to a privacy policy for your Integration that clearly and accurately describes to users of your Integration what user information you collect and how you use and share such information (including for advertising) with HCP and third parties. 5\)Prohibitions and Confidentiality
    a\) API Prohibitions. When using the APIs, you may not (or allow those acting on your behalf to):
        i\) Sublicense or share an API or API Key for use by any third party, other than when requested by a Pro. Consequently, you will not create an API Integration that functions substantially the same as the APIs and offer it for use by third parties.
        ii\) Perform an action with the intent of introducing to HCP products and services any viruses, worms, defects, Trojan horses, malware, or any items of a destructive nature.
        iii\) Interfere with or disrupt the APIs or the servers or networks providing the APIs.
        iv\) Use the API Program for any unauthorized or illegal purpose;
        v\) Defame, abuse, harass, stalk, threaten, intimidate, misrepresent, mislead or otherwise violate the rights (such as, but not limited to, rights of privacy, confidentiality, reputation, and publicity) of others, including Pros, their customers, and HCP staff.
        vi\) Promote or facilitate unlawful online gambling or disruptive commercial messages or advertisements.
        vii\) Publish, post, upload, distribute or disseminate any profane, defamatory, infringing, obscene or unlawful language, material or information.
        viii\) Impersonate another person.
        ix\) Reverse engineer or attempt to extract the source code from any API or any related software, except to the extent that this restriction is expressly prohibited by applicable law.
        x\) Use the APIs for any activities where the use or failure of the APIs could lead to death, personal injury, or environmental damage (such as the operation of nuclear facilities, air traffic control, or life support systems).
        xi\) Collect names, email addresses, phone numbers or other personal information of Pros or their customers by electronic or other means without their express permission
        xii\) Remove, obscure, or alter any HCP terms of service or any links to or notices of those terms.
        xiii\) Cause any third party to engage in the restricted activities above.
    b\) Confidential Matters. Developer credentials (such as passwords, keys, and client IDs) are intended to be used by you and identify your API Integration. You will keep your credentials confidential and make reasonable efforts to prevent and discourage other API Integrations from using your credentials. Developer credentials may not be embedded in open source projects. 6\) DISCLAIMERS.
    a\) WE DO NOT PROMISE THAT THE API PROGRAM (AND ACCESS TO IT) WILL BE ERROR-FREE, UNINTERRUPTED, OR THAT IT WILL PROVIDE SPECIFIC RESULTS. THE API PROGRAM AND ITS CONTENT ARE DELIVERED ON AN “AS-IS” BASIS. WE SPECIFICALLY DISCLAIM ALL WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING ALL IMPLIED WARRANTIES AND CONDITIONS OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, TITLE AND NON-INFRINGEMENT. WE MAKE NO GUARANTEES OF ANY SPECIFIC RESULT FROM USE OF THIS SERVICE. YOUR USE OF THIS SERVICE IS AT YOUR OWN RISK. 7\) You agree to indemnify, defend and hold harmless Housecall Pro and its directors, officers, employees and agents for, from and against all claims and losses of any type, including reasonable attorneys’ fees, in connection with, in whole or in part: (i) any act or omission or willful misconduct by you; (ii) your failure to comply with any applicable federal, state, or local law, or (iii) claims from any of your Customers related to the API Program. 8\) Not a Partnership; Marketing Communications
    a\) You cannot imply or state that you are endorsed by or affiliated with HCP in any manner without express written permission from HCP.
    b\) Any marketing and communications using HCP trade names, trademarks, service marks, logos, domain names, and other distinctive brand features must be reviewed and approved by the HCP team.
    c\) We maintain the right to communicate and market API Developers’ Integrations at HCP’s discretion, in any manner, but with no obligation to do so. 9\) Termination
    a\) Termination. You may stop using our APIs at any time with or without notice. HCP reserves the right to terminate this Agreement with you or discontinue the APIs or any portion or feature or your access thereto for any reason or no reason, and at any time without liability or other obligation to you.
    b\) Your Obligations Post-Termination. Upon any termination of this Agreement or discontinuation of your access to an API, you will immediately stop using the API, cease all use of HCP trade names, trademarks, service marks, logos, domain names, and other distinctive brand features that we previously approved in writing, and delete any cached or stored content unless otherwise approved by HCP in writing. HCP may independently communicate with any of our Pros to provide notice of the termination of your right to use an API.
    c\) Data in Housecall Pro. Upon discontinuation of your access to the API Program, Housecall Pro maintains the right to retain, and use, in perpetuity and in any manner or medium without any financial or other obligations to you, all data originating from the Integration in HCP systems and its Pros accounts. 10\) Non-Solicit. Developers agree that they are prohibited from soliciting or encouraging any Pros to terminate or otherwise change their relationship with Housecall Pro.
